parsed_review.summary: CP00-676 is a descriptor-only RP22 External Integrations I "UI evidence foundation bridge" pack (150 units, RP22.P03.M06.S13–RP22.P04.M03.S20, risk C). Read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the production_ready posture is supported and the pack is correctly in its pre-review state (manifest/inspection/adjudication/command-evidence/claude-review-result all report production_ready=false, status in_progress, pending_review). Directly verified: (1) registry.js:1633-1881 CP676 binding+requirements match the pack baseline exactly — unit_count 150, range, deliverable distribution {test:13,hermes_evidence:7,claude_review:11,implementation:53,contract:17,security_audit:16,ui:30,fixture:3} and micro-title distribution both sum to 150; (2) service.js:2204-2406 descriptor/hermes/claude/handoff functions are descriptor-only with every runtime flag false, Claude read-only and not promoted to final approval, handoff to CP00-677/RP22.P04.M03.S21; (3) validators.js:583-612 and 1273-1380 enforce descriptor_only/no-runtime/no-raw-payload/customer-safe and Claude-not-final, chained into the contract-projection validation; (4) model.test.js:286-296,519-563 assert coverage valid and boundaries closed; (5) both contract files are generated_from_pack_id CP00-676 with a complete no-write/no-real-data attestation (all write/runtime keys false, claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false); (6) index.js re-exports all CP676 symbols; (7) plan live_state and next-pack-queue agree on CP00-676 → RP22.P04.M03.S21. Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic review-packet/evidence-gate handling (H22), not product runtime (runtime_receipt_emitted=false). Claude remains read-only and is not the final approver; the enterprise-trust/human-approval boundary is closed outside this receipt, consistent with the authority boundary. Review-hardening checks pass: readiness auth-preflight succeeded with well-formed JSON (no auth failure, non-zero stdout), read-tools-only mode, no forbidden tools, summary-only correctly prohibited for a risk-C pack with security/review deliverables. Provenance note: the 22/22 validation commands in command-evidence.json (npm test, full validate suite, build, git diff --check) are self-reported by the construction process; as a read-only reviewer I did not re-execute them, but static inspection of the validators, tests, and contracts they exercise corroborates the reported PASS outcomes. One non-blocking P3 (defense-in-depth) is recorded below. No P0/P1/P2 issues; nothing blocks pack or goal closeout.

findings.title: Standalone per-key no-write attestation enforcement covers only cp666_no_write_attestation
findings.details: validateExternalIntegrationsICoreContractProjection applies the standalone per-key false/true enforcement loop (validators.js:1463-1469) and the direct deep-equality check (1460) only to contractProjection.cp666_no_write_attestation. The current pack's cp676_no_write_attestation (service.js:2553) is sourced from the same frozen EXTERNAL_INTEGRATIONS_I_CP666_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION constant and is validated only via full-projection deep-equality when options.expectedProjection is supplied. In the closeout flow this is safe — the script and model test always pass expectedProjection (validate-rp22-external-integrations-i-contract.mjs:318-319; model.test.js:541-563) and the shared frozen constant cannot diverge in the generated projection — so no drift is currently possible. The asymmetry is only that a call without expectedProjection against an externally-supplied contract would independently re-assert cp666's no-write keys but not cp676's.
findings.recommendation: Optionally extend the standalone per-key false/true no-write enforcement to the current-pack attestation (cp676_no_write_attestation) so the boundary is independently re-asserted even when expectedProjection is absent. Non-blocking; current shared-constant + deep-equality coverage already prevents drift in the validated path.
